1	# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
2	# Build the claude-subagent runtime image.
3	# Invoked from REPO ROOT:
4	#   docker build -t ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:test \
5	#                -f images/claude-subagent/Dockerfile .
6	#
7	# Stage 1: compile a static Linux Go binary.
8	FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM golang:1.26-alpine@sha256:a6a091eac01ceac4b97496fe2957a49b6cdd83365337d5f46f6f73710424e805 AS builder
9	ARG TARGETOS
10	ARG TARGETARCH
11	
12	WORKDIR /workspace
13	
14	# Cache the dependency layer before copying source.
15	COPY go.mod go.sum ./
16	RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod go mod download
17	
18	# Copy only what the binary needs (see .dockerignore for exclusions).
19	COPY pkg/dispatch/ pkg/dispatch/
20	COPY internal/harness/ internal/harness/
21	COPY internal/subagent/ internal/subagent/
22	COPY pkg/git/ pkg/git/
23	COPY cmd/claude-subagent/ cmd/claude-subagent/
24	
25	# Static binary: no CGO, stripped symbols for a smaller image.
26	RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/go-build --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod \
27	    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=${TARGETOS:-linux} GOARCH=${TARGETARCH} go build \
28	      -ldflags="-s -w" \
29	      -o /out/claude-subagent \
30	      ./cmd/claude-subagent
31	
32	# Stage 2: Claude Code CLI runtime image.
33	# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 is required because the claude CLI is a Node binary. The CLI
34	# version is pinned (Pitfall 3 — RESEARCH.md: never npm install without an
35	# explicit @<version>); pin tracks the planner's choice on the STACK floor of
36	# v2.1.139. The signed-token credential is injected at K8s Pod-spec time by
37	# the credproxy sidecar (Phase 2 D-C1..C4) — never baked into the image.
38	# Host configuration directories are not bind-mounted (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern).
39	#
40	# git IS required at the executor (task) level: EnsureWorktree shells out to the
41	# git CLI's `git worktree add` (pkg/git/worktree.go — go-git/v5 does not expose
42	# linked worktrees). Clone still uses go-git's pure-Go HTTP transport, but the
43	# per-Task worktree the executor authors into needs the real git binary, so
44	# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 (Debian) gets git installed. Prior to Phase 10 no executor ever
45	# ran end-to-end, so this gap stayed latent.
46	FROM node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732
47	
48	RUN apt-get update \
49	    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git \
50	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
51	    # The per-Project repo (/workspace/repo.git) is cloned by the tide-push Job
52	    # (uid 65532) but the executor runs as uid 1000 — git 2.35+ refuses a repo
53	    # it does not own ("detected dubious ownership"). The pod is the trust
54	    # boundary (hermetic, single-purpose), so mark all repos safe at the system
55	    # level. This covers our `git worktree add` shell-out AND any git the claude
56	    # CLI itself runs inside the worktree (whose .git points back to repo.git).
57	    && git config --system --add safe.directory '*'
58	
59	RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.142
60	
61	COPY --from=builder /out/claude-subagent /usr/local/bin/claude-subagent
62	
63	# Run as non-root UID 1000 — matches D-G3 subagent harness UID requirement.
64	USER 1000
65	
66	ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/claude-subagent"]
67

# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
# Build the claude-subagent runtime image.
# Invoked from REPO ROOT:
#   docker build -t ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:test \
#                -f images/claude-subagent/Dockerfile .
#
# Stage 1: compile a static Linux Go binary.
FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM golang:1.26-alpine@sha256:a6a091eac01ceac4b97496fe2957a49b6cdd83365337d5f46f6f73710424e805 AS builder
ARG TARGETOS
ARG TARGETARCH

WORKDIR /workspace

# Cache the dependency layer before copying source.
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod go mod download

# Copy only what the binary needs (see .dockerignore for exclusions).
COPY pkg/dispatch/ pkg/dispatch/
COPY internal/harness/ internal/harness/
COPY internal/subagent/ internal/subagent/
COPY pkg/git/ pkg/git/
COPY cmd/claude-subagent/ cmd/claude-subagent/

# Static binary: no CGO, stripped symbols for a smaller image.
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/go-build --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod \
    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=${TARGETOS:-linux} GOARCH=${TARGETARCH} go build \
      -ldflags="-s -w" \
      -o /out/claude-subagent \
      ./cmd/claude-subagent

# Stage 2: Claude Code CLI runtime image.
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 is required because the claude CLI is a Node binary. The CLI
# version is pinned (Pitfall 3 — RESEARCH.md: never npm install without an
# explicit @<version>); pin tracks the planner's choice on the STACK floor of
# v2.1.139. The signed-token credential is injected at K8s Pod-spec time by
# the credproxy sidecar (Phase 2 D-C1..C4) — never baked into the image.
# Host configuration directories are not bind-mounted (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern).
#
# git IS required at the executor (task) level: EnsureWorktree shells out to the
# git CLI's `git worktree add` (pkg/git/worktree.go — go-git/v5 does not expose
# linked worktrees). Clone still uses go-git's pure-Go HTTP transport, but the
# per-Task worktree the executor authors into needs the real git binary, so
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 (Debian) gets git installed. Prior to Phase 10 no executor ever
# ran end-to-end, so this gap stayed latent.
FROM node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732

RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    # The per-Project repo (/workspace/repo.git) is cloned by the tide-push Job
    # (uid 65532) but the executor runs as uid 1000 — git 2.35+ refuses a repo
    # it does not own ("detected dubious ownership"). The pod is the trust
    # boundary (hermetic, single-purpose), so mark all repos safe at the system
    # level. This covers our `git worktree add` shell-out AND any git the claude
    # CLI itself runs inside the worktree (whose .git points back to repo.git).
    && git config --system --add safe.directory '*'

RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.142

COPY --from=builder /out/claude-subagent /usr/local/bin/claude-subagent

# Run as non-root UID 1000 — matches D-G3 subagent harness UID requirement.
USER 1000

ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/claude-subagent"]

3

5	you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	
8	    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	
10	Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	limitations under the License.
15	*/
16	
17	package main
18	
19	import (
20		"bytes"
21		"context"
22		"encoding/json"
23		"os"
24		"os/exec"
25		"path/filepath"
26		"strings"
27		"testing"
28	
29		"github.com/go-git/go-git/v5/plumbing"
30		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/subagent/anthropic"
31		pkgdispatch "github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/pkg/dispatch"
32	)
33	
34	// fixtureStreamJSON mirrors the anthropic subagent unit tests — a minimal
35	// stream-json transcript that resolves to a successful EnvelopeOut.
36	const fixtureStreamJSON = `{"type":"system/init","session_id":"sess-claude-subagent"}
37	{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_delta","delta":{"type":"text_delta","text":"ok"}}}
38	{"type":"result","result":"shim-ok","usage":{"input_tokens":10,"output_tokens":5,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0},"total_cost_usd":0.0001}
39	`
40	
41	// withFakeSubagent swaps the package-level newSubagent seam to construct an
42	// anthropic.Anthropic whose exec is a deterministic `bash -c 'cat <fixture>'`.
43	// Restores the seam on test cleanup.
44	func withFakeSubagent(t *testing.T, fixturePath, workspaceRoot string) {
45		t.Helper()
46		orig := newSubagent
47		t.Cleanup(func() { newSubagent = orig })
48		newSubagent = func(claudeBinary, wsRoot string, _ map[string]pkgdispatch.PriceOverride) anthropicRunner {
49			a := anthropic.NewWithExec(
50				anthropic.Options{ClaudeBinary: claudeBinary, WorkspaceRoot: wsRoot},
51				func(ctx context.Context, name string, args ...string) *exec.Cmd {
52					return exec.CommandContext(ctx, "bash", "-c", "cat "+fixturePath)
53				},
54			)
55			_ = workspaceRoot // captured for shape consistency
56			return a
57		}
58	}
59	
60	// writeFixture writes the canned stream-json transcript to <dir>/fixture.jsonl
61	// and returns the absolute path.
62	func writeFixture(t *testing.T, dir, content string) string {
63		t.Helper()
64		path := filepath.Join(dir, "fixture.jsonl")
65		if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0o644); err != nil {
66			t.Fatalf("write fixture: %v", err)
67		}
68		return path
69	}
70	
71	// writeEnvelopeInFile marshals env to JSON and drops it at envelopePath,
72	// creating parent dirs.
73	func writeEnvelopeInFile(t *testing.T, envelopePath string, env pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn) {
74		t.Helper()
75		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(envelopePath), 0o755); err != nil {
76			t.Fatalf("mkdir envelope dir: %v", err)
77		}
78		data, err := json.Marshal(env)
79		if err != nil {
80			t.Fatalf("marshal envelope-in: %v", err)
81		}
82		if err := os.WriteFile(envelopePath, data, 0o644); err != nil {
83			t.Fatalf("write envelope-in: %v", err)
84		}
85	}
86	
87	// TestClaudeSubagentMain_HappyPath asserts the shim loads EnvelopeIn, runs
88	// anthropic via the fake exec, and writes a populated out.json — exit 0.
89	// (Plan 03-07 Task 1 Test 1.)
90	func TestClaudeSubagentMain_HappyPath(t *testing.T) {
91		tmp := t.TempDir()
92		fixturePath := writeFixture(t, tmp, fixtureStreamJSON)
93		withFakeSubagent(t, fixturePath, tmp)
94	
95		envelopePath := filepath.Join(tmp, "envelopes", "t-1", "in.json")
96		env := pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn{
97			APIVersion: pkgdispatch.APIVersionV1Alpha1,
98			Kind:       pkgdispatch.KindTaskEnvelopeIn,
99			TaskUID:    "t-1",
100			Role:       "planner",
101			Level:      "milestone",
102			Prompt:     "hello",
103			Provider: pkgdispatch.ProviderSpec{
104				Vendor: "anthropic",
105				Model:  "claude-sonnet-4-6",
106			},
107			ProxyEndpoint: "https://127.0.0.1:8443",
108			SignedToken:   "fixture-token",
109		}
110		writeEnvelopeInFile(t, envelopePath, env)
111	
112		var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
113		code := run(context.Background(), envelopePath, tmp, &stdout, &stderr)
114		if code != 0 {
115			t.Fatalf("exit code: got %d, want 0; stderr=%s", code, stderr.String())
116		}
117		// Verify out.json exists at sibling path and contains a valid EnvelopeOut.
118		outPath := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(envelopePath), "out.json")
119		data, err := os.ReadFile(outPath)
120		if err != nil {
121			t.Fatalf("read out.json: %v", err)
122		}
123		var got pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut
124		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &got); err != nil {
125			t.Fatalf("unmarshal out.json: %v", err)
126		}
127		if got.TaskUID != "t-1" {
128			t.Errorf("TaskUID: got %q, want %q", got.TaskUID, "t-1")
129		}
130		if got.ExitCode != 0 {
131			t.Errorf("EnvelopeOut.ExitCode: got %d, want 0", got.ExitCode)
132		}
133		if got.Result == "" {
134			t.Errorf("EnvelopeOut.Result is empty; want parsed result text")
135		}
136		if got.Usage.InputTokens != 10 || got.Usage.OutputTokens != 5 {
137			t.Errorf("Usage: got %+v, want In=10 Out=5", got.Usage)
138		}
139	}
140	
141	// TestClaudeSubagentMain_EnvelopeLoadFailure asserts that a missing envelope
142	// path produces exit 2 and a stderr containing "envelope". (Plan 03-07 Task 1
143	// Test 2.)
144	func TestClaudeSubagentMain_EnvelopeLoadFailure(t *testing.T) {
145		tmp := t.TempDir()
146		// Make newSubagent panic if called — the shim must not invoke it when
147		// envelope load fails.
148		orig := newSubagent
149		t.Cleanup(func() { newSubagent = orig })
150		newSubagent = func(claudeBinary, wsRoot string, _ map[string]pkgdispatch.PriceOverride) anthropicRunner {
151			t.Fatalf("newSubagent must not be invoked on envelope load failure")
152			return nil
153		}
154	
155		bogusPath := filepath.Join(tmp, "does-not-exist", "in.json")
156		var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
157		code := run(context.Background(), bogusPath, tmp, &stdout, &stderr)
158		if code != 2 {
159			t.Fatalf("exit code: got %d, want 2; stderr=%s", code, stderr.String())
160		}
161		if !strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(stderr.String()), "envelope") {
162			t.Errorf("stderr should mention envelope; got %q", stderr.String())
163		}
164	}
165	
166	// TestClaudeSubagentMain_VendorMismatch asserts that an envelope with
167	// Provider.Vendor != "anthropic" causes anthropic.Run() to return a
168	// dispatch-level error; the shim must wrap that error as a failure-shaped
169	// EnvelopeOut, persist it to out.json, and return a non-zero exit code.
170	// (Plan 03-07 Task 1 Test 3.)
171	func TestClaudeSubagentMain_VendorMismatch(t *testing.T) {
172		tmp := t.TempDir()
173		fixturePath := writeFixture(t, tmp, fixtureStreamJSON)
174		withFakeSubagent(t, fixturePath, tmp)
175	
176		envelopePath := filepath.Join(tmp, "envelopes", "t-vendor", "in.json")
177		env := pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn{
178			APIVersion: pkgdispatch.APIVersionV1Alpha1,
179			Kind:       pkgdispatch.KindTaskEnvelopeIn,
180			TaskUID:    "t-vendor",
181			Role:       "planner",
182			Level:      "milestone",
183			Provider: pkgdispatch.ProviderSpec{
184				Vendor: "openai", // intentional mismatch — anthropic refuses.
185				Model:  "gpt-4",
186			},
187		}
188		writeEnvelopeInFile(t, envelopePath, env)
189	
190		var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
191		code := run(context.Background(), envelopePath, tmp, &stdout, &stderr)
192		if code == 0 {
193			t.Fatalf("expected non-zero exit on vendor mismatch, got 0; stderr=%s", stderr.String())
194		}
195		// out.json must exist with a failure-shaped EnvelopeOut.
196		outPath := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(envelopePath), "out.json")
197		data, err := os.ReadFile(outPath)
198		if err != nil {
199			t.Fatalf("read out.json: %v", err)
200		}
201		var got pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut
202		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &got); err != nil {
203			t.Fatalf("unmarshal out.json: %v", err)
204		}
205		if got.ExitCode == 0 {
206			t.Errorf("EnvelopeOut.ExitCode: got 0 on vendor mismatch, want != 0")
207		}
208		if got.Result == "" && got.Reason == "" {
209			t.Errorf("EnvelopeOut should describe the failure in Result or Reason; got empty")
210		}
211	}
212	
213	// TestClaudeSubagentMain_InvokesEnsureWorktreeBeforeRun asserts the shim
214	// calls EnsureWorktree BEFORE the subagent's Run() — order matters because
215	// the subagent's prompt assumes the worktree dir already exists. We assert
216	// via a recorded call order: ensureWorktreeFunc bumps a counter to 1; the
217	// fake newSubagent sees counter==1 when its Run is invoked. (Plan 03-07
218	// Task 3 Test 5 — cross-file integration; ties Task 3 to Task 1's shim.)
219	func TestClaudeSubagentMain_InvokesEnsureWorktreeBeforeRun(t *testing.T) {
220		tmp := t.TempDir()
221		fixturePath := writeFixture(t, tmp, fixtureStreamJSON)
222		// Mark the order seen at each call site.
223		var order []string
224		origEW := ensureWorktreeFunc
225		t.Cleanup(func() { ensureWorktreeFunc = origEW })
226		ensureWorktreeFunc = func(in pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn, workspaceRoot, branch string) error {
227			order = append(order, "ensure-worktree")
228			return nil
229		}
230		origSA := newSubagent
231		t.Cleanup(func() { newSubagent = origSA })
232		newSubagent = func(claudeBinary, wsRoot string, _ map[string]pkgdispatch.PriceOverride) anthropicRunner {
233			return anthropic.NewWithExec(
234				anthropic.Options{ClaudeBinary: claudeBinary, WorkspaceRoot: wsRoot},
235				func(ctx context.Context, name string, args ...string) *exec.Cmd {
236					order = append(order, "subagent-run")
237					return exec.CommandContext(ctx, "bash", "-c", "cat "+fixturePath)
238				},
239			)
240		}
241		// Override the commit seam — this test exercises call ordering, not commit behavior.
242		origCW := commitWorktreeFunc
243		t.Cleanup(func() { commitWorktreeFunc = origCW })
244		commitWorktreeFunc = func(worktreeDir, taskUID string) (plumbing.Hash, bool, error) {
245			return plumbing.NewHash("aabbccdd" + strings.Repeat("0", 32)), false, nil
246		}
247	
248		envelopePath := filepath.Join(tmp, "envelopes", "t-order", "in.json")
249		env := pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn{
250			APIVersion: pkgdispatch.APIVersionV1Alpha1,
251			Kind:       pkgdispatch.KindTaskEnvelopeIn,
252			TaskUID:    "t-order",
253			Role:       "executor",
254			Level:      "task",
255			Provider:   pkgdispatch.ProviderSpec{Vendor: "anthropic", Model: "claude-sonnet-4-6"},
256		}
257		writeEnvelopeInFile(t, envelopePath, env)
258	
259		var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
260		code := run(context.Background(), envelopePath, tmp, &stdout, &stderr)
261		if code != 0 {
262			t.Fatalf("exit code: got %d, want 0; stderr=%s", code, stderr.String())
263		}
264		if len(order) < 2 {
265			t.Fatalf("expected ≥2 ordered calls; got %v", order)
266		}
267		if order[0] != "ensure-worktree" {
268			t.Errorf("call order: got %v, want ensure-worktree first", order)
269		}
270		if order[1] != "subagent-run" {
271			t.Errorf("call order: got %v, want subagent-run second", order)
272		}
273	}
274	
275	// TestClaudeSubagentMain_PassesEnvBranchToWorktree is the 09-09 regression guard:
276	// the executor's worktree branch must come from EnvelopeIn.Branch (in.json), not the
277	// never-written branch.txt. A previous build passed an empty branch → EnsureWorktree
278	// failed with "git worktree: empty branch" on every real-Claude task.
279	func TestClaudeSubagentMain_PassesEnvBranchToWorktree(t *testing.T) {
280		tmp := t.TempDir()
281		fixturePath := writeFixture(t, tmp, fixtureStreamJSON)
282		const wantBranch = "tide/run-medium-project-1780956333"
283	
284		var gotBranch string
285		origEW := ensureWorktreeFunc
286		t.Cleanup(func() { ensureWorktreeFunc = origEW })
287		ensureWorktreeFunc = func(in pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn, workspaceRoot, branch string) error {
288			gotBranch = branch
289			return nil
290		}
291		origSA := newSubagent
292		t.Cleanup(func() { newSubagent = origSA })
293		newSubagent = func(claudeBinary, wsRoot string, _ map[string]pkgdispatch.PriceOverride) anthropicRunner {
294			return anthropic.NewWithExec(
295				anthropic.Options{ClaudeBinary: claudeBinary, WorkspaceRoot: wsRoot},
296				func(ctx context.Context, name string, args ...string) *exec.Cmd {
297					return exec.CommandContext(ctx, "bash", "-c", "cat "+fixturePath)
298				},
299			)
300		}
301		// Override the commit seam — this test exercises branch-passing, not commit behavior.
302		origCW := commitWorktreeFunc
303		t.Cleanup(func() { commitWorktreeFunc = origCW })
304		commitWorktreeFunc = func(worktreeDir, taskUID string) (plumbing.Hash, bool, error) {
305			return plumbing.NewHash("aabbccdd" + strings.Repeat("0", 32)), false, nil
306		}
307	
308		envelopePath := filepath.Join(tmp, "envelopes", "t-branch", "in.json")
309		env := pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn{
310			APIVersion: pkgdispatch.APIVersionV1Alpha1,
311			Kind:       pkgdispatch.KindTaskEnvelopeIn,
312			TaskUID:    "t-branch",
313			Role:       "executor",
314			Level:      "task",
315			Branch:     wantBranch,
316			Provider:   pkgdispatch.ProviderSpec{Vendor: "anthropic", Model: "claude-sonnet-4-6"},
317		}
318		writeEnvelopeInFile(t, envelopePath, env)
319	
320		var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
321		if code := run(context.Background(), envelopePath, tmp, &stdout, &stderr); code != 0 {
322			t.Fatalf("exit code: got %d, want 0; stderr=%s", code, stderr.String())
323		}
324		if gotBranch != wantBranch {
325			t.Errorf("worktree branch = %q, want %q (must come from EnvelopeIn.Branch, not branch.txt)", gotBranch, wantBranch)
326		}
327	}
328	
329	// TestClaudeSubagentMain_IgnoresDevTestMode asserts that even when env.Dev is
330	// populated, the shim does NOT switch on Dev.TestMode — it always goes
331	// through anthropic.New().Run(). Behavior is identical to the happy-path.
332	// (Plan 03-07 Task 1 Test 4 — anti-pattern enforcement: real Claude image
333	// MUST ignore env.Dev entirely per PATTERNS.md line 442.)
334	func TestClaudeSubagentMain_IgnoresDevTestMode(t *testing.T) {
335		tmp := t.TempDir()
336		fixturePath := writeFixture(t, tmp, fixtureStreamJSON)
337		withFakeSubagent(t, fixturePath, tmp)
338	
339		envelopePath := filepath.Join(tmp, "envelopes", "t-dev", "in.json")
340		env := pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn{
341			APIVersion: pkgdispatch.APIVersionV1Alpha1,
342			Kind:       pkgdispatch.KindTaskEnvelopeIn,
343			TaskUID:    "t-dev",
344			Role:       "planner",
345			Level:      "milestone",
346			Provider: pkgdispatch.ProviderSpec{
347				Vendor: "anthropic",
348				Model:  "claude-sonnet-4-6",
349			},
350			Dev: &pkgdispatch.Dev{TestMode: "success"},
351		}
352		writeEnvelopeInFile(t, envelopePath, env)
353	
354		var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
355		code := run(context.Background(), envelopePath, tmp, &stdout, &stderr)
356		if code != 0 {
357			t.Fatalf("exit code: got %d, want 0; stderr=%s", code, stderr.String())
358		}
359		// Same shape as TestClaudeSubagentMain_HappyPath — proves Dev was ignored
360		// and the fake-exec produced the canned anthropic stream.
361		outPath := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(envelopePath), "out.json")
362		data, err := os.ReadFile(outPath)
363		if err != nil {
364			t.Fatalf("read out.json: %v", err)
365		}
366		var got pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut
367		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &got); err != nil {
368			t.Fatalf("unmarshal out.json: %v", err)
369		}
370		if got.Result == "" {
371			t.Errorf("EnvelopeOut.Result is empty; if the shim had switched on Dev.TestMode it might have synthesized success — we want the fake-exec result instead")
372		}
373	}

33	// initGitWorktreeDir creates a minimal git repo at dir with a single commit
34	// so the executor's CommitWorktree step can run successfully (HEAD exists).
35	func initGitWorktreeDir(t *testing.T, dir string) {
36		t.Helper()
37		cmds := [][]string{
38			{"git", "-C", dir, "init", "-q"},
39			{"git", "-C", dir, "config", "user.email", "test@tide.local"},
40			{"git", "-C", dir, "config", "user.name", "tide-test"},
41			{"git", "-C", dir, "commit", "-q", "--allow-empty", "-m", "initial"},
42		}
43		for _, c := range cmds {
44			if out, err := exec.Command(c[0], c[1:]...).CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
45				t.Fatalf("setup %v: %v\n%s", c, err, out)
46			}
47		}
48	}
49	
50	// withFakeSubagentSuccess swaps the newSubagent seam to return a fake that
51	// always exits 0 using a canned transcript. Restores on test cleanup.
52	func withFakeSubagentSuccess(t *testing.T, tmp string) {
53		t.Helper()
54		fixturePath := writeFixture(t, tmp, fixtureStreamJSON)
55		orig := newSubagent
56		t.Cleanup(func() { newSubagent = orig })
57		newSubagent = func(claudeBinary, wsRoot string, _ map[string]pkgdispatch.PriceOverride) anthropicRunner {
58			return anthropic.NewWithExec(
59				anthropic.Options{ClaudeBinary: claudeBinary, WorkspaceRoot: wsRoot},
60				func(ctx context.Context, name string, args ...string) *exec.Cmd {
61					return exec.CommandContext(ctx, "bash", "-c", "cat "+fixturePath)
62				},
63			)
64		}
65	}
66	
67	// TestRunCommitsExecutorWorktree asserts that run() calls CommitWorktree after
68	// the subagent exits 0 when Role="executor", and out.Git.HeadSHA is non-empty.
69	func TestRunCommitsExecutorWorktree(t *testing.T) {
70		tmp := t.TempDir()
71	
72		// Prepare the executor worktree directory with an uncommitted file.
73		worktreeDir := filepath.Join(tmp, "worktrees", "task-exec-01")
74		if err := os.MkdirAll(worktreeDir, 0o755); err != nil {
75			t.Fatalf("mkdir worktreeDir: %v", err)
76		}
77		initGitWorktreeDir(t, worktreeDir)
78		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(worktreeDir, "authored.go"), []byte("package main\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
79			t.Fatalf("write file: %v", err)
80		}
81	
82		withFakeSubagentSuccess(t, tmp)
83	
84		// Skip the real EnsureWorktree; the worktree already exists.
85		origEW := ensureWorktreeFunc
86		t.Cleanup(func() { ensureWorktreeFunc = origEW })
87		ensureWorktreeFunc = func(in pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn, workspaceRoot, branch string) error { return nil }
88	
89		envelopePath := filepath.Join(tmp, "envelopes", "task-exec-01", "in.json")
90		env := pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn{
91			APIVersion: pkgdispatch.APIVersionV1Alpha1,
92			Kind:       pkgdispatch.KindTaskEnvelopeIn,
93			TaskUID:    "task-exec-01",
94			Role:       "executor",
95			Level:      "task",
96			Provider:   pkgdispatch.ProviderSpec{Vendor: "anthropic", Model: "claude-sonnet-4-6"},
97		}
98		writeEnvelopeInFile(t, envelopePath, env)
99	
100		var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
101		code := run(context.Background(), envelopePath, tmp, &stdout, &stderr)
102		if code != 0 {
103			t.Fatalf("exit code: got %d, want 0; stderr=%s", code, stderr.String())
104		}
105	
106		// Read out.json and verify Git.HeadSHA is populated.
107		outPath := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(envelopePath), "out.json")
108		data, err := os.ReadFile(outPath)
109		if err != nil {
110			t.Fatalf("read out.json: %v", err)
111		}
112		var got pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut
113		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &got); err != nil {
114			t.Fatalf("unmarshal out.json: %v", err)
115		}
116		if got.Git == nil || got.Git.HeadSHA == "" {
117			t.Errorf("out.Git.HeadSHA: got empty/nil, want a non-empty SHA (executor role + non-empty diff)")
118		}
119		if got.ExitCode != 0 {
120			t.Errorf("ExitCode: got %d, want 0", got.ExitCode)
121		}
122	}
123	
124	// TestRunEmptyDiffOverridesExitCode asserts that when the executor worktree
125	// has no changes after the subagent exits 0, run() returns exit code 1 and
126	// out.Result == "empty-diff" (D-03 / SC-2 policy).
127	func TestRunEmptyDiffOverridesExitCode(t *testing.T) {
128		tmp := t.TempDir()
129	
130		// Executor worktree exists but has no uncommitted changes.
131		worktreeDir := filepath.Join(tmp, "worktrees", "task-empty-01")
132		if err := os.MkdirAll(worktreeDir, 0o755); err != nil {
133			t.Fatalf("mkdir worktreeDir: %v", err)
134		}
135		initGitWorktreeDir(t, worktreeDir)
136	
137		withFakeSubagentSuccess(t, tmp)
138	
139		origEW := ensureWorktreeFunc
140		t.Cleanup(func() { ensureWorktreeFunc = origEW })
141		ensureWorktreeFunc = func(in pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn, workspaceRoot, branch string) error { return nil }
142	
143		envelopePath := filepath.Join(tmp, "envelopes", "task-empty-01", "in.json")
144		env := pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn{
145			APIVersion: pkgdispatch.APIVersionV1Alpha1,
146			Kind:       pkgdispatch.KindTaskEnvelopeIn,
147			TaskUID:    "task-empty-01",
148			Role:       "executor",
149			Level:      "task",
150			Provider:   pkgdispatch.ProviderSpec{Vendor: "anthropic", Model: "claude-sonnet-4-6"},
151		}
152		writeEnvelopeInFile(t, envelopePath, env)
153	
154		var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
155		code := run(context.Background(), envelopePath, tmp, &stdout, &stderr)
156		if code != 1 {
157			t.Fatalf("exit code: got %d, want 1 (empty-diff must be an explicit failure)", code)
158		}
159	
160		outPath := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(envelopePath), "out.json")
161		data, err := os.ReadFile(outPath)
162		if err != nil {
163			t.Fatalf("read out.json: %v", err)
164		}
165		var got pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut
166		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &got); err != nil {
167			t.Fatalf("unmarshal out.json: %v", err)
168		}
169		if got.Result != "empty-diff" {
170			t.Errorf("Result: got %q, want \"empty-diff\"", got.Result)
171		}
172		if got.ExitCode != 1 {
173			t.Errorf("ExitCode: got %d, want 1", got.ExitCode)
174		}
175	}
176	
177	// TestRunPlannerSkipsCommit asserts that CommitWorktree is NOT called when
178	// Role="planner", and that ExitCode=0 from the runner is preserved.
179	func TestRunPlannerSkipsCommit(t *testing.T) {
180		tmp := t.TempDir()
181	
182		withFakeSubagentSuccess(t, tmp)
183	
184		origEW := ensureWorktreeFunc
185		t.Cleanup(func() { ensureWorktreeFunc = origEW })
186		ensureWorktreeFunc = func(in pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn, workspaceRoot, branch string) error { return nil }
187	
188		commitCalled := false
189		origCW := commitWorktreeFunc
190		t.Cleanup(func() { commitWorktreeFunc = origCW })
191		commitWorktreeFunc = func(worktreeDir, taskUID string) (plumbing.Hash, bool, error) {
192			commitCalled = true
193			return plumbing.ZeroHash, false, nil
194		}
195	
196		envelopePath := filepath.Join(tmp, "envelopes", "task-plan-01", "in.json")
197		env := pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn{
198			APIVersion: pkgdispatch.APIVersionV1Alpha1,
199			Kind:       pkgdispatch.KindTaskEnvelopeIn,
200			TaskUID:    "task-plan-01",
201			Role:       "planner",
202			Level:      "milestone",
203			Provider:   pkgdispatch.ProviderSpec{Vendor: "anthropic", Model: "claude-sonnet-4-6"},
204		}
205		writeEnvelopeInFile(t, envelopePath, env)
206	
207		var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
208		code := run(context.Background(), envelopePath, tmp, &stdout, &stderr)
209		if code != 0 {
210			t.Fatalf("exit code: got %d, want 0; stderr=%s", code, stderr.String())
211		}
212		if commitCalled {
213			t.Error("commitWorktreeFunc should NOT be called for planner role")
214		}
215	}

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Working Rules

Project-agnostic operating principles. Apply these before anything else, including before reading the rest of this file.

### 1. Observe First

Every wasted iteration in Phase 02.2 traced back to acting on assumptions instead of observations. The cascade-5 debug session hypothesized a "Wave Ready check" that didn't exist; the cascade-8 debug session hypothesized "ResourceVersion-conflict exponential-backoff suppression" when the actual cause was a never-assigned `Dispatcher` field. Both were avoidable.

1. When a runtime gate is BLOCKED → **read the manager log (`kubectl logs` or `/tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log`) and the VERIFICATION.md frontmatter BEFORE hypothesizing**
2. When a controller misbehaves → **`kubectl logs` + `kubectl describe <crd>` + `kubectl get <crd> -o yaml` BEFORE reading reconciler source**
3. When a debug hypothesis cites a specific code path → **grep the codebase to confirm it exists in the cited form** (`grep -nE "Wave.*Ready|Ready.*Wave" internal/controller/` would have refuted cascade-5's framing in seconds)
4. When a code change has no visible effect → **verify the binary rebuilt (image digest changed) and the Pod rotated (`kubectl get pods -n tide-system` shows new age)**, don't keep tweaking
5. When suggesting a Makefile target → **`grep -nE '^target-name:' Makefile`** to confirm it exists before recommending the command
6. When an agent could answer a question → **but `kubectl get`, `git log --grep`, or a single grep answers it faster, use the CLI**

Subagents are for understanding architecture and authoring artifacts. Runtime artifacts (manager logs, CRD status, git history) are for debugging runtime failures. Do not confuse the two.

### 2. Execute, Don't Ask

When the next step is obvious and non-destructive AND inside an active GSD workflow: do it. Don't present the command and ask for confirmation. This includes running `make test-int`, rerunning failing tests, creating planning artifacts, applying fixes specified in an approved `PLAN.md`, and committing per the executor's task protocol.

**Exceptions — always confirm or refuse:**

- **Production code edits outside an active GSD plan.** The `GSD Workflow Enforcement` section below is binding. Even if the fix is obvious, route through `/gsd:quick` or `/gsd:debug` so artifacts stay in sync.
- **Edits to `charts/tide/values.yaml`** — chart is FIXED contract per Phase 02.2's anti-pattern. Binary catches up to chart, never reverse.
- **`git push --force`** to any branch, especially `main`.
- **`git reset --hard`** outside a worktree's own HEAD-correction step.
- **`kind delete cluster`** outside an explicit clean-rerun sequence (it destroys all test state).
- **`rm -rf`** on `.git/`, `.planning/`, `.claude/`, or any directory containing uncommitted user work.
- **`gsd-sdk query state-set ...`** that downgrades phase status or rewrites tracked progress fields.

### 3. Verify Before Claiming

Never say "this should fix it," "the fix is complete," or "cascade-N is closed." Run the verification. Read the gate_decision. Grep for the artifact. Then state what you observed.

Concrete verification protocols for Phase 02.2-style runtime gates:

- **Plan landed:** `git log --oneline -1 -- <files_modified>` shows the expected commit on `main`
- **VERIFICATION.md exists:** `ls .planning/phases/<dir>/<plan>-VERIFICATION.md` returns a file
- **Gate APPROVED:** `grep -cE '^gate_decision: APPROVED$' <VERIFICATION>` returns exactly `1`
- **Test ran:** `tail -30 /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` shows Ginkgo summary line `Ran X Specs in Ys`
- **Dispatch is live (Phase 02.2-10 evidence shape):** `grep -E '"creating job"|"dispatch"' /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` returns ≥ 1 line, AND `kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces` shows Jobs in the test namespaces
- **Cascade closed:** the specific manager-log error that defined the cascade (e.g. `"no project found in namespace X"`) returns 0 in the new run's log
- **`make test-int` exit ≠ "Ginkgo green."** The `test/integration/kind` package bundles plain go-tests (e.g. the helm-template contract tests like `TestHelmDeploymentTemplateRendersManagerPodAnnotations`) alongside the Ginkgo specs. One RED go-test fails the package and trips `make test-int` non-zero **even when both layers print `Ran X of Y … SUCCESS!`**. Always read the echoed `MAKE_EXIT` AND `grep -nE '^--- FAIL|^FAIL\s'` the log — not just the Ginkgo summary. (Phase 7 nearly tagged v1.0 on "14/14 green" while `make test-int` was red on a dropped chart-template block.)
- **A subagent's "pre-existing / unrelated" dismissal of a failing test is a claim, not verification.** Before relaying it as safe, git-archaeology the regressing commit (`git log -S '<token>' -- <file>`) and confirm it does not fail the gate. (Two Phase-7 agents waved off the dropped `podAnnotations` render block as pre-existing; it was a real ship-blocker — the verifier caught it and a `make test-int` re-run proved it.)

## Operating Notes (Phase 02.2 lessons)

Rules learned from 10 BLOCKED iterations across Phase 02.2. Trust them.

- **Don't predict "chain terminator."** Plans 02.2-08/09/10 each predicted the end; all surfaced new cascades. Frame work as iterations; don't anchor on terminations.
- **Pre-empt worktree cwd-drift before merging.** Executor agents occasionally write artifacts to main's working tree instead of the worktree's. Pre-merge check:
  ```
  UNTRACKED=$(git status --porcelain | grep '^??' | grep "<plan>-" || true)
  ```
  If non-empty, diff against the worktree branch's commits; remove duplicates before `git merge`. (Plan 02.2-09 merge failed without this; Plan 02.2-10 succeeded with it.)
- **`gsd-sdk query state.begin-phase` resets STATE.md body fields.** It re-derives `Current Position` and `Status` from current plan inventory. Edit body fields AFTER `state.begin-phase` runs, not before, or the edit gets clobbered.
- **Question implicit framings when cascade classes diverge.** If 3+ consecutive cascades are the same class (e.g. cascades 2/3 both flag-mismatches), trust tactical iteration. If consecutive cascades differ in class (e.g. cascade-4 budget → cascade-5 harness-bug → cascade-6 timeouts), surface "is the underlying assumption still right?" before authoring the next plan. The cascade-8 debug session forced this question 7 plans late.
- **Use `--wave N` filter when a closeout plan is gated on a BLOCKED artifact.** Plan 02.2-02's `checkpoint:human-verify` reads `gate_decision: APPROVED` from a verification artifact; `--auto` mode would false-approve against a BLOCKED gate. Filter dispatch with `--wave <new-gap-wave>` until any plan records APPROVED and the rewire fires.
- **Subagent prompts: focus, don't restate.** Agents have their own system prompts that cover anti-patterns and protocols. `<additional_context>` blocks should carry decision-only context (the specific gap, the user's choice, the corrected hypothesis) — not the full cascade history or the entire anti-pattern table. Token budget directly affects subagent quality.
- **Honor `workflow.auto_advance: true` literally** — fire the chain across phase boundaries. But ASK FIRST at fix-shape decision points (Option A vs B at scope-defining moments). The user memory note covers this; respect it.
- **Decorative banners are noise unless they signal a real workflow transition.** `━━━ GSD ► STAGE ━━━` belongs at PLANNING / VERIFYING / EXECUTING boundaries (per `references/ui-brand.md`), not on every status update. Routine progress can be one-line.
- **End-of-turn summary: one or two sentences.** What changed and what's next. Resist the urge to recap multi-section status when the conversation thread is fresh.
- **Use TaskCreate for multi-cycle workflows.** A plan-check-commit-execute loop repeated 5+ times in one session genuinely benefits from task tracking. The system reminders about TaskCreate are not boilerplate.
- **STATE.md "Current Position" body text drifts.** It's prose, not derived; updates to it need to happen explicitly. The frontmatter (`progress.completed_plans`) is computed by SDK verbs; the body text is not.
- **Constrained-VM full-suite recipe (the dev Docker VM is ~7.65 GiB).** It DOES fit a clean `make test-int` if you don't accumulate state: delete → recreate → pre-warm (provisioner Ready + `kind load busybox:1.36`) a fresh kind cluster per heavy run, one heavy run at a time. Never let `make acceptance-v1-smoke` (it spins its OWN `tide-acceptance-<ts>` cluster) run while a `tide-test` cluster is still up — two single-node clusters OOM the node (exit 137). Phase 7's "env-gated, needs a bigger VM" deferral was actually solvable this way on the same 7.65 GiB host.
- **The acceptance `$0` path is a different deploy surface than Layer B — peel it one layer at a time.** Layer B fixtures carry a dummy `providerSecretRef` and use the test harness's helm install; the `$0` small-project has none and uses the chart defaults. So `$0` acceptance surfaces bugs Layer B masks (cascade-12 chart `default "latest"` image tags; cascade-13 credproxy hard-requiring `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`). Expect each green layer to expose the next; don't predict the terminator.

## What this repository is

**TIDE** (Topologically-Indexed Dependency Execution) — a Kubernetes-native orchestrator for hierarchical agentic coding work, designed to be open-sourced and to run against any project/codebase in any cluster. `README.md` is the design spec (and doubles as the public-facing README); check `git log` and `.planning/` for current implementation state.

## The spec is load-bearing

Everything downstream — schemas, APIs, controller logic, persistence — should trace back to the paradigm doc. When designing or implementing, preserve these distinctions; they exist for reasons the doc argues for explicitly:

The cascade-5 debug session hypothesized a "Wave Ready check" that didn't exist; the cascade-8 debug session hypothesized "ResourceVersion-conflict exponential-backoff suppression" when the actual cause was a never-assigned `Dispatcher` field. Both were avoidable.

1. When a runtime gate is BLOCKED → **read the manager log (`kubectl logs` or `/tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log`) and the VERIFICATION.md frontmatter BEFORE hypothesizing**
2. When a controller misbehaves → **`kubectl logs` + `kubectl describe <crd>` + `kubectl get <crd> -o yaml` BEFORE reading reconciler source**
3. When a debug hypothesis cites a specific code path → **grep the codebase to confirm it exists in the cited form** (`grep -nE "Wave.*Ready|Ready.*Wave" internal/controller/` would have refuted cascade-5's framing in seconds)
4. When a code change has no visible effect → **verify the binary rebuilt (image digest changed) and the Pod rotated (`kubectl get pods -n tide-system` shows new age)**, don't keep tweaking
5. When suggesting a Makefile target → **`grep -nE '^target-name:' Makefile`** to confirm it exists before recommending the command
6. When an agent could answer a question → **but `kubectl get`, `git log --grep`, or a single grep answers it faster, use the CLI**

Subagents are for understanding architecture and authoring artifacts. Runtime artifacts (manager logs, CRD status, git history) are for debugging runtime failures. Do not confuse the two.

### 2. Execute, Don't Ask

When the next step is obvious and non-destructive AND inside an active GSD workflow: do it. Don't present the command and ask for confirmation. This includes running `make test-int`, rerunning failing tests, creating planning artifacts, applying fixes specified in an approved `PLAN.md`, and committing per the executor's task protocol.

**Exceptions — always confirm or refuse:**

- **Production code edits outside an active GSD plan.** The `GSD Workflow Enforcement` section below is binding. Even if the fix is obvious, route through `/gsd:quick` or `/gsd:debug` so artifacts stay in sync.
- **Edits to `charts/tide/values.yaml`** — chart is FIXED contract per Phase 02.2's anti-pattern. Binary catches up to chart, never reverse.
- **`git push --force`** to any branch, especially `main`.
- **`git reset --hard`** outside a worktree's own HEAD-correction step.
- **`kind delete cluster`** outside an explicit clean-rerun sequence (it destroys all test state).
- **`rm -rf`** on `.git/`, `.planning/`, `.claude/`, or any directory containing uncommitted user work.
- **`gsd-sdk query state-set ...`** that downgrades phase status or rewrites tracked progress fields.

### 3. Verify Before Claiming

Never say "this should fix it," "the fix is complete," or "cascade-N is closed." Run the verification. Read the gate_decision. Grep for the artifact. Then state what you observed.

Concrete verification protocols for Phase 02.2-style runtime gates:

- **Plan landed:** `git log --oneline -1 -- <files_modified>` shows the expected commit on `main`
- **VERIFICATION.md exists:** `ls .planning/phases/<dir>/<plan>-VERIFICATION.md` returns a file
- **Gate APPROVED:** `grep -cE '^gate_decision: APPROVED$' <VERIFICATION>` returns exactly `1`
- **Test ran:** `tail -30 /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` shows Ginkgo summary line `Ran X Specs in Ys`
- **Dispatch is live (Phase 02.2-10 evidence shape):** `grep -E '"creating job"|"dispatch"' /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` returns ≥ 1 line, AND `kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces` shows Jobs in the test namespaces
- **Cascade closed:** the specific manager-log error that defined the cascade (e.g. `"no project found in namespace X"`) returns 0 in the new run's log
- **`make test-int` exit ≠ "Ginkgo green."** The `test/integration/kind` package bundles plain go-tests (e.g. the helm-template contract tests like `TestHelmDeploymentTemplateRendersManagerPodAnnotations`) alongside the Ginkgo specs. One RED go-test fails the package and trips `make test-int` non-zero **even when both layers print `Ran X of Y … SUCCESS!`**. Always read the echoed `MAKE_EXIT` AND `grep -nE '^--- FAIL|^FAIL\s'` the log — not just the Ginkgo summary. (Phase 7 nearly tagged v1.0 on "14/14 green" while `make test-int` was red on a dropped chart-template block.)
- **A subagent's "pre-existing / unrelated" dismissal of a failing test is a claim, not verification.** Before relaying it as safe, git-archaeology the regressing commit (`git log -S '<token>' -- <file>`) and confirm it does not fail the gate. (Two Phase-7 agents waved off the dropped `podAnnotations` render block as pre-existing; it was a real ship-blocker — the verifier caught it and a `make test-int` re-run proved it.)

## Operating Notes (Phase 02.2 lessons)

Rules learned from 10 BLOCKED iterations across Phase 02.2. Trust them.

- **Don't predict "chain terminator."** Plans 02.2-08/09/10 each predicted the end; all surfaced new cascades. Frame work as iterations; don't anchor on terminations.
- **Pre-empt worktree cwd-drift before merging.** Executor agents occasionally write artifacts to main's working tree instead of the worktree's. Pre-merge check:
  ```
  UNTRACKED=$(git status --porcelain | grep '^??' | grep "<plan>-" || true)
  ```
  If non-empty, diff against the worktree branch's commits; remove duplicates before `git merge`. (Plan 02.2-09 merge failed without this; Plan 02.2-10 succeeded with it.)
- **`gsd-sdk query state.begin-phase` resets STATE.md body fields.** It re-derives `Current Position` and `Status` from current plan inventory. Edit body fields AFTER `state.begin-phase` runs, not before, or the edit gets clobbered.
- **Question implicit framings when cascade classes diverge.** If 3+ consecutive cascades are the same class (e.g. cascades 2/3 both flag-mismatches), trust tactical iteration. If consecutive cascades differ in class (e.g. cascade-4 budget → cascade-5 harness-bug → cascade-6 timeouts), surface "is the underlying assumption still right?" before authoring the next plan. The cascade-8 debug session forced this question 7 plans late.
- **Use `--wave N` filter when a closeout plan is gated on a BLOCKED artifact.** Plan 02.2-02's `checkpoint:human-verify` reads `gate_decision: APPROVED` from a verification artifact; `--auto` mode would false-approve against a BLOCKED gate. Filter dispatch with `--wave <new-gap-wave>` until any plan records APPROVED and the rewire fires.
- **Subagent prompts: focus, don't restate.** Agents have their own system prompts that cover anti-patterns and protocols. `<additional_context>` blocks should carry decision-only context (the specific gap, the user's choice, the corrected hypothesis) — not the full cascade history or the entire anti-pattern table. Token budget directly affects subagent quality.
- **Honor `workflow.auto_advance: true` literally** — fire the chain across phase boundaries. But ASK FIRST at fix-shape decision points (Option A vs B at scope-defining moments). The user memory note covers this; respect it.
- **Decorative banners are noise unless they signal a real workflow transition.** `━━━ GSD ► STAGE ━━━` belongs at PLANNING / VERIFYING / EXECUTING boundaries (per `references/ui-brand.md`), not on every status update. Routine progress can be one-line.
- **End-of-turn summary: one or two sentences.** What changed and what's next. Resist the urge to recap multi-section status when the conversation thread is fresh.
- **Use TaskCreate for multi-cycle workflows.** A plan-check-commit-execute loop repeated 5+ times in one session genuinely benefits from task tracking. The system reminders about TaskCreate are not boilerplate.
- **STATE.md "Current Position" body text drifts.** It's prose, not derived; updates to it need to happen explicitly. The frontmatter (`progress.completed_plans`) is computed by SDK verbs; the body text is not.
- **Constrained-VM full-suite recipe (the dev Docker VM is ~7.65 GiB).** It DOES fit a clean `make test-int` if you don't accumulate state: delete → recreate → pre-warm (provisioner Ready + `kind load busybox:1.36`) a fresh kind cluster per heavy run, one heavy run at a time. Never let `make acceptance-v1-smoke` (it spins its OWN `tide-acceptance-<ts>` cluster) run while a `tide-test` cluster is still up — two single-node clusters OOM the node (exit 137). Phase 7's "env-gated, needs a bigger VM" deferral was actually solvable this way on the same 7.65 GiB host.
- **The acceptance `$0` path is a different deploy surface than Layer B — peel it one layer at a time.** Layer B fixtures carry a dummy `providerSecretRef` and use the test harness's helm install; the `$0` small-project has none and uses the chart defaults. So `$0` acceptance surfaces bugs Layer B masks (cascade-12 chart `default "latest"` image tags; cascade-13 credproxy hard-requiring `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`). Expect each green layer to expose the next; don't predict the terminator.

## What this repository is

**TIDE** (Topologically-Indexed Dependency Execution) — a Kubernetes-native orchestrator for hierarchical agentic coding work, designed to be open-sourced and to run against any project/codebase in any cluster. `README.md` is the design spec (and doubles as the public-facing README); check `git log` and `.planning/` for current implementation state.

## The spec is load-bearing

Everything downstream — schemas, APIs, controller logic, persistence — should trace back to the paradigm doc. When designing or implementing, preserve these distinctions; they exist for reasons the doc argues for explicitly:

# Phase Brief — phase-03-codex-image-entrypoint

**Phase:** phase-03-codex-image-entrypoint  
**Milestone:** milestone-03-hetero-integration  
**Depends on:** phase-02-codex-subagent-core  
**Status:** Planned

---

## Objective

Deliver the runtime image and entrypoint shim that make `internal/subagent/codex/`
dispatchable as a container — the final bridge between the Phase 02 Go package and a
running K8s Job pod. Phase 02 proved the codex subagent works as a library; this phase
produces the binary (`cmd/codex-subagent/`) that wraps it and the multi-stage Dockerfile
that bundles it together with the Codex CLI, giving the controller a pullable image ref
(`ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent`) to schedule.

Nothing in this phase touches the controller, the chart, or any v1alpha2 API type.
The provider firewall (`make verify-import-firewall`, `verify-dispatch-imports`) must
remain green — all Codex/OpenAI-specific code stays behind `pkg/dispatch.Subagent`.

---

## Scope

**In scope:**
- `cmd/codex-subagent/main.go` — thin shim mirroring `cmd/claude-subagent/main.go`:
  load `EnvelopeIn` via `harness.ReadEnvelopeIn` → `codex.Run` → write `EnvelopeOut`
  via `harness.WriteEnvelopeOut` + termination stub. Vendor sentinel is `"openai"`.
  No `Dev.TestMode` branching (PATTERNS.md line 442 — real subagent images ignore
  `env.Dev` entirely). Executor role calls `harness.CommitWorktree` after a zero exit;
  planner role skips commit.
- `cmd/codex-subagent/main_test.go` — offline unit tests round-tripping fixture JSONL
  envelopes through the shim; mirrors `cmd/claude-subagent/main_test.go` test topology
  (HappyPath, EnvelopeLoadFailure, VendorMismatch, InvokesEnsureWorktreeBeforeRun,
  PassesEnvBranchToWorktree, IgnoresDevTestMode). Uses a fake `codex` binary seam
  (`newSubagent` package-level var) — no live OpenAI calls.
- `cmd/codex-subagent/commit_test.go` — executor commit + empty-diff tests mirroring
  `cmd/claude-subagent/commit_test.go` (TestRunCommitsExecutorWorktree,
  TestRunEmptyDiffOverridesExitCode, TestRunPlannerSkipsCommit).
- `images/codex-subagent/Dockerfile` — multi-stage build mirroring
  `images/claude-subagent/Dockerfile`: Stage 1 compiles a static Linux Go binary from
  `cmd/codex-subagent/`; Stage 2 installs Node + the pinned `@openai/codex` npm
  package (Codex CLI), copies the Go binary, runs as UID 1000. Canonical image ref:
  `ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent`.
- `Makefile` additions — `docker-build-codex-subagent` and
  `docker-push-codex-subagent` targets mirroring the existing `claude-subagent`
  targets; `IMAGE_TAG` convention unchanged.

**Out of scope (handled by other phases):**
- Controller Helm chart wiring (phase-05-chart-secret-wiring).
- Per-level vendor switch in `ResolveProvider` (phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch).
- Heterogeneous integration test (phase-06-hetero-integration).

---

## Expected Deliverables

1. `cmd/codex-subagent/main.go` — harness-wrapped entrypoint; `run()` is the testable
   seam; `newSubagent` is the injectable fake seam.
2. `cmd/codex-subagent/main_test.go` — six offline unit tests, all green under
   `make test` without `OPENAI_API_KEY`.
3. `cmd/codex-subagent/commit_test.go` — three executor-path unit tests mirroring the
   claude-subagent commit battery.
4. `images/codex-subagent/Dockerfile` — multi-stage; `docker build` clean from repo
   root (`docker build -t ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent:test -f images/codex-subagent/Dockerfile .`).
5. `Makefile` — `docker-build-codex-subagent` / `docker-push-codex-subagent` targets.

---

## Verification Gates

| Gate | Criterion |
|------|-----------|
| G1 | `make test` exits 0 — all existing + new unit tests pass offline |
| G2 | `make verify-import-firewall` green — `internal/subagent/codex` not imported by `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/` |
| G3 | `make verify-dispatch-imports` green — no OpenAI SDK leak into controller/manager |
| G4 | `docker build -t ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent:test -f images/codex-subagent/Dockerfile .` exits 0 |
| G5 | Shim round-trips a fixture `EnvelopeIn{Provider.Vendor:"openai"}` to `EnvelopeOut{ExitCode:0}` offline (TestCodexSubagentMain_HappyPath) |
| G6 | Shim rejects `Provider.Vendor:"anthropic"` with non-zero exit + failure-shaped `out.json` (TestCodexSubagentMain_VendorMismatch) |
| G7 | No `OPENAI_API_KEY` required for any test in the suite (offline guard) |

---

## Interface Contracts (locked by MILESTONE.md — do not reinvent)

- Binary entrypoint resolves `TIDE_TASK_UID` → `/workspace/envelopes/<uid>/in.json`
  (mirrors the claude-subagent default; controller passes no `--envelope-path`).
- Vendor sentinel string: `"openai"` (matches phase-02's `codex.vendorSentinel`).
- Image ref convention: `ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent` (phase-05 wires
  this into the Helm chart).
- No `Dev.TestMode` branch — this binary is a real runtime, not the stub.
- `TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON` read via the same `parsePricingOverridesFromEnv`
  helper pattern (Codex-side pricing table, not the anthropic one).
EOF
echo "Phase brief written"

input.new_string:
# Phase Brief — phase-03-codex-image-entrypoint

**Phase:** phase-03-codex-image-entrypoint
**Milestone:** milestone-03-hetero-integration
**Depends on:** phase-02-codex-subagent-core

---

## Objective

Deliver the runtime image and entrypoint shim that make `internal/subagent/codex/`
dispatchable as a container — the final bridge between the Phase 02 Go package and a
running K8s Job pod. Phase 02 proves the codex subagent works as a library; this phase
produces the binary (`cmd/codex-subagent/`) that wraps it and the multi-stage Dockerfile
that bundles it with the Codex CLI, giving the controller a pullable image ref
(`ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent`) to schedule.

Nothing in this phase touches the controller, the chart, or any v1alpha2 API type.
The provider firewall (`make verify-import-firewall`, `verify-dispatch-imports`) must
remain green — all Codex/OpenAI-specific code stays behind `pkg/dispatch.Subagent`.

---

## Scope

**In scope:**

- `cmd/codex-subagent/main.go` — thin shim mirroring `cmd/claude-subagent/main.go`:
  load `EnvelopeIn` via `harness.ReadEnvelopeIn`, call `codex.Run`, write `EnvelopeOut`
  via `harness.WriteEnvelopeOut` + termination stub. Vendor sentinel is `"openai"`.
  No `Dev.TestMode` branching (PATTERNS.md line 442 — real subagent images ignore
  `env.Dev` entirely). Executor role calls `harness.CommitWorktree` after zero exit;
  planner role skips commit.

- `cmd/codex-subagent/main_test.go` — six offline unit tests round-tripping fixture JSONL
  envelopes through the shim; mirrors `cmd/claude-subagent/main_test.go` test topology
  (HappyPath, EnvelopeLoadFailure, VendorMismatch, InvokesEnsureWorktreeBeforeRun,
  PassesEnvBranchToWorktree, IgnoresDevTestMode). Uses the `newSubagent` package-level
  fake seam — no live OpenAI calls, no `OPENAI_API_KEY` required.

- `cmd/codex-subagent/commit_test.go` — executor commit + empty-diff tests mirroring
  `cmd/claude-subagent/commit_test.go` (TestRunCommitsExecutorWorktree,
  TestRunEmptyDiffOverridesExitCode, TestRunPlannerSkipsCommit).

- `images/codex-subagent/Dockerfile` — multi-stage build mirroring
  `images/claude-subagent/Dockerfile`. Stage 1: static Linux Go binary compiled from
  `cmd/codex-subagent/` via `CGO_ENABLED=0`. Stage 2: Node slim base, pinned
  `@openai/codex` npm package (Codex CLI), Git installed, Go binary copied, UID 1000.
  Canonical image ref: `ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent`.

- `Makefile` additions — `docker-build-codex-subagent` and `docker-push-codex-subagent`
  targets mirroring the existing claude-subagent targets; `IMAGE_TAG` convention unchanged;
  wired into the umbrella `docker-build` / `docker-push` goals.

**Out of scope (handled by other phases):**

- Helm chart wiring, Secret injection (phase-05-chart-secret-wiring).
- Per-level vendor switch in `ResolveProvider` (phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch).
- Heterogeneous integration test (phase-06-hetero-integration).
- `internal/subagent/codex/` package itself (phase-02-codex-subagent-core).

---

## Expected Deliverables

1. `cmd/codex-subagent/main.go` — harness-wrapped entrypoint; `run()` testable seam;
   `newSubagent` injectable fake seam.
2. `cmd/codex-subagent/main_test.go` — six offline unit tests, all green under `make test`
   without `OPENAI_API_KEY`.
3. `cmd/codex-subagent/commit_test.go` — three executor-path unit tests.
4. `images/codex-subagent/Dockerfile` — multi-stage; `docker build` clean from repo root.
5. `Makefile` additions — `docker-build-codex-subagent` / `docker-push-codex-subagent`.

---

## Verification Gates

| Gate | Criterion |
|------|-----------|
| G1 | `make test` exits 0 — all existing and new unit tests pass offline |
| G2 | `make verify-import-firewall` green — `internal/subagent/codex` not in `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/` |
| G3 | `make verify-dispatch-imports` green — no OpenAI SDK leak into controller/manager |
| G4 | `docker build -t ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent:test -f images/codex-subagent/Dockerfile .` exits 0 |
| G5 | Shim round-trips `EnvelopeIn{Provider.Vendor:"openai"}` to `EnvelopeOut{ExitCode:0}` offline |
| G6 | Shim rejects `Provider.Vendor:"anthropic"` with non-zero exit + failure-shaped `out.json` |
| G7 | No `OPENAI_API_KEY` required for any test — suite passes fully offline |

---

## Wave Decomposition (illustrative — authoritative schedule derived by Kahn over Task DAG)

**Wave 1 — independent:**
- `plan-01-codex-entrypoint-shim` — `cmd/codex-subagent/` Go binary + all tests

**Wave 2 — depends on plan-01:**
- `plan-02-codex-dockerfile` — `images/codex-subagent/Dockerfile` + `Makefile` targets

---

## Interface Contracts (locked by MILESTONE.md — do not reinvent)

- Envelope default path: `/workspace/envelopes/<TIDE_TASK_UID>/in.json`
- Vendor sentinel string: `"openai"` — matches phase-02's `codex.vendorSentinel`
- Image ref: `ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent` — phase-05 wires into Helm chart
- No `Dev.TestMode` branch — real runtime binary, not the stub
- `TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON` parsed via `parsePricingOverridesFromEnv` (Codex pricing table)

tool_input.command:
cat > /workspace/envelopes/3abdba95-26a1-4d06-a095-fb1c029604e7/children/phase-brief.md << 'EOF'
# Phase Brief — phase-03-codex-image-entrypoint

**Phase:** phase-03-codex-image-entrypoint  
**Milestone:** milestone-03-hetero-integration  
**Depends on:** phase-02-codex-subagent-core  
**Status:** Planned

---

## Objective

Deliver the runtime image and entrypoint shim that make `internal/subagent/codex/`
dispatchable as a container — the final bridge between the Phase 02 Go package and a
running K8s Job pod. Phase 02 proved the codex subagent works as a library; this phase
produces the binary (`cmd/codex-subagent/`) that wraps it and the multi-stage Dockerfile
that bundles it together with the Codex CLI, giving the controller a pullable image ref
(`ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent`) to schedule.

Nothing in this phase touches the controller, the chart, or any v1alpha2 API type.
The provider firewall (`make verify-import-firewall`, `verify-dispatch-imports`) must
remain green — all Codex/OpenAI-specific code stays behind `pkg/dispatch.Subagent`.

---

## Scope

**In scope:**
- `cmd/codex-subagent/main.go` — thin shim mirroring `cmd/claude-subagent/main.go`:
  load `EnvelopeIn` via `harness.ReadEnvelopeIn` → `codex.Run` → write `EnvelopeOut`
  via `harness.WriteEnvelopeOut` + termination stub. Vendor sentinel is `"openai"`.
  No `Dev.TestMode` branching (PATTERNS.md line 442 — real subagent images ignore
  `env.Dev` entirely). Executor role calls `harness.CommitWorktree` after a zero exit;
  planner role skips commit.
- `cmd/codex-subagent/main_test.go` — offline unit tests round-tripping fixture JSONL
  envelopes through the shim; mirrors `cmd/claude-subagent/main_test.go` test topology
  (HappyPath, EnvelopeLoadFailure, VendorMismatch, InvokesEnsureWorktreeBeforeRun,
  PassesEnvBranchToWorktree, IgnoresDevTestMode). Uses a fake `codex` binary seam
  (`newSubagent` package-level var) — no live OpenAI calls.
- `cmd/codex-subagent/commit_test.go` — executor commit + empty-diff tests mirroring
  `cmd/claude-subagent/commit_test.go` (TestRunCommitsExecutorWorktree,
  TestRunEmptyDiffOverridesExitCode, TestRunPlannerSkipsCommit).
- `images/codex-subagent/Dockerfile` — multi-stage build mirroring
  `images/claude-subagent/Dockerfile`: Stage 1 compiles a static Linux Go binary from
  `cmd/codex-subagent/`; Stage 2 installs Node + the pinned `@openai/codex` npm
  package (Codex CLI), copies the Go binary, runs as UID 1000. Canonical image ref:
  `ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent`.
- `Makefile` additions — `docker-build-codex-subagent` and
  `docker-push-codex-subagent` targets mirroring the existing `claude-subagent`
  targets; `IMAGE_TAG` convention unchanged.

**Out of scope (handled by other phases):**
- Controller Helm chart wiring (phase-05-chart-secret-wiring).
- Per-level vendor switch in `ResolveProvider` (phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch).
- Heterogeneous integration test (phase-06-hetero-integration).

---

## Expected Deliverables

1. `cmd/codex-subagent/main.go` — harness-wrapped entrypoint; `run()` is the testable
   seam; `newSubagent` is the injectable fake seam.
2. `cmd/codex-subagent/main_test.go` — six offline unit tests, all green under
   `make test` without `OPENAI_API_KEY`.
3. `cmd/codex-subagent/commit_test.go` — three executor-path unit tests mirroring the
   claude-subagent commit battery.
4. `images/codex-subagent/Dockerfile` — multi-stage; `docker build` clean from repo
   root (`docker build -t ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent:test -f images/codex-subagent/Dockerfile .`).
5. `Makefile` — `docker-build-codex-subagent` / `docker-push-codex-subagent` targets.

---

## Verification Gates

| Gate | Criterion |
|------|-----------|
| G1 | `make test` exits 0 — all existing + new unit tests pass offline |
| G2 | `make verify-import-firewall` green — `internal/subagent/codex` not imported by `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/` |
| G3 | `make verify-dispatch-imports` green — no OpenAI SDK leak into controller/manager |
| G4 | `docker build -t ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent:test -f images/codex-subagent/Dockerfile .` exits 0 |
| G5 | Shim round-trips a fixture `EnvelopeIn{Provider.Vendor:"openai"}` to `EnvelopeOut{ExitCode:0}` offline (TestCodexSubagentMain_HappyPath) |
| G6 | Shim rejects `Provider.Vendor:"anthropic"` with non-zero exit + failure-shaped `out.json` (TestCodexSubagentMain_VendorMismatch) |
| G7 | No `OPENAI_API_KEY` required for any test in the suite (offline guard) |

---

## Interface Contracts (locked by MILESTONE.md — do not reinvent)

- Binary entrypoint resolves `TIDE_TASK_UID` → `/workspace/envelopes/<uid>/in.json`
  (mirrors the claude-subagent default; controller passes no `--envelope-path`).
- Vendor sentinel string: `"openai"` (matches phase-02's `codex.vendorSentinel`).
- Image ref convention: `ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent` (phase-05 wires
  this into the Helm chart).
- No `Dev.TestMode` branch — this binary is a real runtime, not the stub.
- `TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON` read via the same `parsePricingOverridesFromEnv`
  helper pattern (Codex-side pricing table, not the anthropic one).
EOF
echo "Phase brief written"
